# solution.py
"""TeamSyncPro -- a collaborative schedule and task management system.

This module implements the back-end engine for TeamSyncPro (exposed through
the :class:`TeamCollaborationManager` facade). It provides a thread-safe,
in-memory domain model that lets multiple team members:

* register, update and manage user accounts (collaboration roster),
* create projects with start/end windows,
* create, assign, reassign and track tasks with deadlines and a clear
  NOT_STARTED -> IN_PROGRESS -> COMPLETED lifecycle,
* communicate through threaded messages with file attachments scoped to a
  project or a task,
* measure productivity (completion rate, average completion time, peer
  ratings, per-user dashboards),
* generate project / team / individual reports and export them to CSV or PDF.

Design highlights
-----------------
* **Real-time consistency under load** -- every mutating and reading
  operation is guarded by a single re-entrant lock shared by every internal
  store, so concurrent clients always observe a consistent view and identifiers
  never collide.
* **Globally unique, monotonic identifiers** -- a single counter (starting at
  ``1``) hands out ids across every entity type.
* **Defensive copies** -- accessors return deep copies so callers can never
  mutate engine-owned state by accident.
* **Adaptive task management** -- assignment indexes allow O(1)-ish reassignment
  and rebalancing of work between team members.

Only the Python standard library is used and all output is ASCII.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import csv
import io
import threading
from collections import defaultdict
from copy import deepcopy
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum
from typing import Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple

__all__ = [
    "TeamCollaborationManager",
    "TaskStatus",
    "UserRole",
    "ReportFormat",
    "ReportType",
    "TeamCollaborationError",
    "ValidationError",
    "NotFoundError",
    "AuthorizationError",
    "DuplicateError",
    "ConflictError",
    "Attachment",
    "User",
    "Project",
    "Task",
    "Message",
    "Rating",
]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enumerations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TaskStatus(Enum):
    """Lifecycle states a task can move through."""

    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class UserRole(Enum):
    """Authorization role for a team member."""

    MEMBER = "member"
    ADMIN = "admin"


class ReportType(Enum):
    """The kind of analytical report produced by the engine."""

    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE = "individual_performance"


class ReportFormat(Enum):
    """Supported export formats for a report."""

    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exception hierarchy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for every error raised by the engine."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when input data fails a validation rule."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not allowed to perform an operation."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint would be violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with the current system state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain entities
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class User:
    """A team member able to own projects and be assigned tasks."""

    id: int
    username: str
    email: str
    role: UserRole = UserRole.MEMBER
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Project:
    """A bounded body of work owned by a user and containing tasks."""

    id: int
    name: str
    start_date: datetime
    end_date: datetime
    owner_id: int
    description: str = ""
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Task:
    """A unit of work with a deadline, an assignee and a status lifecycle."""

    id: int
    project_id: int
    title: str
    status: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
    assignee_id: Optional[int] = None
    deadline: Optional[datetime] = None
    started_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    completed_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Attachment:
    """File metadata attached to a message (content size only, no payload)."""

    filename: str
    size: int
    uploaded_by: int
    uploaded_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Message:
    """A threaded message scoped to exactly one project or one task."""

    id: int
    author_id: int
    body: str
    project_id: Optional[int] = None
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    parent_id: Optional[int] = None
    attachments: List[Attachment] = field(default_factory=list)
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    edited_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Rating:
    """Peer feedback (1-5) optionally tied to a specific task."""

    id: int
    rater_id: int
    ratee_id: int
    score: int
    feedback: str = ""
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Internal stores -- thin data holders that share one lock
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _BaseStore:
    """Base store; every store shares the manager's single lock instance."""

    def __init__(self, lock: threading.RLock) -> None:
        self._lock = lock


class _UserStore(_BaseStore):
    def __init__(self, lock: threading.RLock) -> None:
        super().__init__(lock)
        self._items: Dict[int, User] = {}
        self._by_username: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self._by_email: Dict[str, int] = {}


class _ProjectStore(_BaseStore):
    def __init__(self, lock: threading.RLock) -> None:
        super().__init__(lock)
        self._items: Dict[int, Project] = {}


class _TaskStore(_BaseStore):
    def __init__(self, lock: threading.RLock) -> None:
        super().__init__(lock)
        self._items: Dict[int, Task] = {}
        self._by_project: Dict[int, List[int]] = defaultdict(list)
        self._by_assignee: Dict[int, set] = defaultdict(set)


class _MessageStore(_BaseStore):
    def __init__(self, lock: threading.RLock) -> None:
        super().__init__(lock)
        self._items: Dict[int, Message] = {}
        self._by_project: Dict[int, List[int]] = defaultdict(list)
        self._by_task: Dict[int, List[int]] = defaultdict(list)


class _PerformanceStore(_BaseStore):
    def __init__(self, lock: threading.RLock) -> None:
        super().__init__(lock)
        self._items: Dict[int, Rating] = {}
        self._by_ratee: Dict[int, List[Rating]] = defaultdict(list)


class _ReportStore(_BaseStore):
    """Stateless store; reporting reads from the other stores under the lock."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Facade / engine
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationManager:
    """Thread-safe facade exposing the full TeamSyncPro feature set."""

    def __init__(self, now: Optional[Callable[[], datetime]] = None) -> None:
        self._now: Callable[[], datetime] = now if now is not None else datetime.now
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        self._id_counter = 0

        # Every store shares the single lock so the engine behaves as one
        # consistent unit under concurrency.
        self.users = _UserStore(self._lock)
        self.projects = _ProjectStore(self._lock)
        self.tasks = _TaskStore(self._lock)
        self.messages = _MessageStore(self._lock)
        self.performance = _PerformanceStore(self._lock)
        self.reports = _ReportStore(self._lock)

    # -- identifiers -------------------------------------------------------
    def _next_id(self) -> int:
        with self._lock:
            self._id_counter += 1
            return self._id_counter

    # -- internal lookups (assume lock held) -------------------------------
    def _require_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        user = self.users._items.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))
        return user

    def _require_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        project = self.projects._items.get(project_id)
        if project is None:
            raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))
        return project

    def _require_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        task = self.tasks._items.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("task {0} not found".format(task_id))
        return task

    def _require_message(self, message_id: int) -> Message:
        message = self.messages._items.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            raise NotFoundError("message {0} not found".format(message_id))
        return message

    # ==================================================================
    # User management
    # ==================================================================
    def register_user(
        self, username: str, email: str, role: UserRole = UserRole.MEMBER
    ) -> User:
        """Register a new team member and return a copy of the record."""
        username = self._clean_username(username)
        email = self._clean_email(email)
        if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
            raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")

        with self._lock:
            if username in self.users._by_username:
                raise DuplicateError("username already in use")
            if email in self.users._by_email:
                raise DuplicateError("email already in use")

            user = User(
                id=self._next_id(),
                username=username,
                email=email,
                role=role,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.users._items[user.id] = user
            self.users._by_username[username] = user.id
            self.users._by_email[email] = user.id
            return deepcopy(user)

    def get_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        """Return a copy of the user or raise :class:`NotFoundError`."""
        with self._lock:
            return deepcopy(self._require_user(user_id))

    def list_users(self) -> List[User]:
        """Return all users as copies, sorted by id."""
        with self._lock:
            return [
                deepcopy(self.users._items[uid])
                for uid in sorted(self.users._items)
            ]

    def update_user(
        self,
        user_id: int,
        username: Optional[str] = None,
        email: Optional[str] = None,
        role: Optional[UserRole] = None,
    ) -> User:
        """Update mutable user fields, enforcing uniqueness and validation."""
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)

            new_username = user.username
            if username is not None:
                new_username = self._clean_username(username)
                existing = self.users._by_username.get(new_username)
                if existing is not None and existing != user_id:
                    raise DuplicateError("username already in use")

            new_email = user.email
            if email is not None:
                new_email = self._clean_email(email)
                existing = self.users._by_email.get(new_email)
                if existing is not None and existing != user_id:
                    raise DuplicateError("email already in use")

            if role is not None and not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")

            # Apply username / email index changes.
            if new_username != user.username:
                self.users._by_username.pop(user.username, None)
                self.users._by_username[new_username] = user_id
                user.username = new_username
            if new_email != user.email:
                self.users._by_email.pop(user.email, None)
                self.users._by_email[new_email] = user_id
                user.email = new_email
            if role is not None:
                user.role = role
            return deepcopy(user)

    def delete_user(self, user_id: int) -> None:
        """Delete a user; blocked while they own projects or open tasks."""
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)

            for project in self.projects._items.values():
                if project.owner_id == user_id:
                    raise ConflictError("user owns one or more projects")

            for task_id in self.tasks._by_assignee.get(user_id, set()):
                task = self.tasks._items.get(task_id)
                if task is not None and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    raise ConflictError("user has open assigned tasks")

            self.users._by_username.pop(user.username, None)
            self.users._by_email.pop(user.email, None)
            self.users._items.pop(user_id, None)

    # ==================================================================
    # Project management
    # ==================================================================
    def create_project(
        self,
        name: str,
        start_date: datetime,
        end_date: datetime,
        owner_id: int,
        description: str = "",
    ) -> Project:
        """Create a project; ``end_date`` may equal but not precede start."""
        name = self._clean_text(name, "project name")
        if end_date < start_date:
            raise ValidationError("end_date must not precede start_date")

        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(owner_id)
            project = Project(
                id=self._next_id(),
                name=name,
                start_date=start_date,
                end_date=end_date,
                owner_id=owner_id,
                description=description or "",
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.projects._items[project.id] = project
            return deepcopy(project)

    def get_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            return deepcopy(self._require_project(project_id))

    def list_projects(self) -> List[Project]:
        with self._lock:
            return [
                deepcopy(self.projects._items[pid])
                for pid in sorted(self.projects._items)
            ]

    def update_project(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        name: Optional[str] = None,
        start_date: Optional[datetime] = None,
        end_date: Optional[datetime] = None,
        description: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)

            new_name = project.name
            if name is not None:
                new_name = self._clean_text(name, "project name")

            new_start = start_date if start_date is not None else project.start_date
            new_end = end_date if end_date is not None else project.end_date
            if new_end < new_start:
                raise ValidationError("end_date must not precede start_date")

            project.name = new_name
            project.start_date = new_start
            project.end_date = new_end
            if description is not None:
                project.description = description
            return deepcopy(project)

    def delete_project(self, project_id: int) -> None:
        """Delete a project and cascade to its tasks and messages."""
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)

            for task_id in list(self.tasks._by_project.get(project_id, [])):
                task = self.tasks._items.pop(task_id, None)
                if task is not None and task.assignee_id is not None:
                    self.tasks._by_assignee[task.assignee_id].discard(task_id)
            self.tasks._by_project.pop(project_id, None)

            for message_id in list(self.messages._by_project.get(project_id, [])):
                self.messages._items.pop(message_id, None)
            self.messages._by_project.pop(project_id, None)

            self.projects._items.pop(project_id, None)

    # ==================================================================
    # Task management
    # ==================================================================
    def create_task(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        title: str,
        assignee_id: Optional[int] = None,
        deadline: Optional[datetime] = None,
    ) -> Task:
        title = self._clean_text(title, "task title")
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._require_user(assignee_id)
            if deadline is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                raise ValidationError("deadline must not precede project start")

            task = Task(
                id=self._next_id(),
                project_id=project_id,
                title=title,
                status=TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                assignee_id=assignee_id,
                deadline=deadline,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.tasks._items[task.id] = task
            self.tasks._by_project[project_id].append(task.id)
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self.tasks._by_assignee[assignee_id].add(task.id)
            return deepcopy(task)

    def get_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            return deepcopy(self._require_task(task_id))

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            ids = sorted(self.tasks._by_project.get(project_id, []))
            return [deepcopy(self.tasks._items[tid]) for tid in ids]

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            ids = sorted(self.tasks._by_assignee.get(user_id, set()))
            return [deepcopy(self.tasks._items[tid]) for tid in ids]

    def assign_task(self, task_id: int, assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
            self._set_assignee(task, assignee_id)
            return deepcopy(task)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id: int, new_assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(new_assignee_id)
            self._set_assignee(task, new_assignee_id)
            return deepcopy(task)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._set_assignee(task, None)
            return deepcopy(task)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id: int, deadline: Optional[datetime]) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                project = self._require_project(task.project_id)
                if deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError(
                        "deadline must not precede project start"
                    )
            task.deadline = deadline
            return deepcopy(task)

    def update_status(self, task_id: int, status: TaskStatus) -> Task:
        if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
            raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus")
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            now = self._now()
            if status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now
            else:  # NOT_STARTED
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None
            task.status = status
            return deepcopy(task)

    def _set_assignee(self, task: Task, new_assignee_id: Optional[int]) -> None:
        """Move a task between assignee indexes (lock held by caller)."""
        if task.assignee_id is not None:
            self.tasks._by_assignee[task.assignee_id].discard(task.id)
        if new_assignee_id is not None:
            self.tasks._by_assignee[new_assignee_id].add(task.id)
        task.assignee_id = new_assignee_id

    # ==================================================================
    # Messaging / communication
    # ==================================================================
    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id: int,
        body: str,
        project_id: Optional[int] = None,
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
        attachments: Optional[List[Tuple[str, bytes]]] = None,
        parent_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> Message:
        """Post a message to exactly one project or task channel."""
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(author_id)

            if (project_id is None) == (task_id is None):
                raise ValidationError(
                    "exactly one of project_id or task_id is required"
                )
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)

            has_attachments = bool(attachments)
            if not self._has_text(body) and not has_attachments:
                raise ValidationError("message body or attachment required")

            if parent_id is not None:
                self._require_message(parent_id)

            built = self._build_attachments(attachments, author_id)
            message = Message(
                id=self._next_id(),
                author_id=author_id,
                body=body,
                project_id=project_id,
                task_id=task_id,
                parent_id=parent_id,
                attachments=built,
                created_at=self._now(),
                edited_at=None,
            )
            self.messages._items[message.id] = message
            if project_id is not None:
                self.messages._by_project[project_id].append(message.id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self.messages._by_task[task_id].append(message.id)
            return deepcopy(message)

    def edit_message(
        self, message_id: int, editor_id: int, new_body: str
    ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != editor_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may edit a message")
            if not self._has_text(new_body):
                raise ValidationError("message body required")
            message.body = new_body
            message.edited_at = self._now()
            return deepcopy(message)

    def add_attachment(
        self, message_id: int, uploader_id: int, filename: str, data: bytes
    ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != uploader_id:
                raise AuthorizationError(
                    "only the author may add attachments"
                )
            message.attachments.append(
                self._make_attachment(filename, data, uploader_id)
            )
            return deepcopy(message)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            ids = sorted(self.messages._by_project.get(project_id, []))
            return [deepcopy(self.messages._items[mid]) for mid in ids]

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_task(task_id)
            ids = sorted(self.messages._by_task.get(task_id, []))
            return [deepcopy(self.messages._items[mid]) for mid in ids]

    def delete_message(self, message_id: int, requester_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != requester_id:
                raise AuthorizationError(
                    "only the author may delete a message"
                )
            if message.project_id is not None:
                self._discard(
                    self.messages._by_project.get(message.project_id), message_id
                )
            if message.task_id is not None:
                self._discard(
                    self.messages._by_task.get(message.task_id), message_id
                )
            self.messages._items.pop(message_id, None)

    def _build_attachments(
        self, attachments: Optional[List[Tuple[str, bytes]]], uploader_id: int
    ) -> List[Attachment]:
        built: List[Attachment] = []
        for item in attachments or []:
            filename, data = item
            built.append(self._make_attachment(filename, data, uploader_id))
        return built

    def _make_attachment(
        self, filename: str, data: bytes, uploader_id: int
    ) -> Attachment:
        if filename is None or not str(filename).strip():
            raise ValidationError("attachment filename required")
        size = len(data) if data is not None else 0
        return Attachment(
            filename=filename,
            size=size,
            uploaded_by=uploader_id,
            uploaded_at=self._now(),
        )

    @staticmethod
    def _discard(seq: Optional[List[int]], value: int) -> None:
        if seq is not None and value in seq:
            seq.remove(value)

    # ==================================================================
    # Performance dashboard
    # ==================================================================
    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id: int) -> float:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._user_tasks(user_id)
            if not tasks:
                return 0.0
            completed = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            return completed / len(tasks)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id: int) -> Optional[float]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            durations = self._completion_durations(self._user_tasks(user_id))
            if not durations:
                return None
            return sum(durations) / len(durations)

    def submit_rating(
        self,
        rater_id: int,
        ratee_id: int,
        score: int,
        feedback: str = "",
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> Rating:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(rater_id)
            self._require_user(ratee_id)
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("users cannot rate themselves")
            if not isinstance(score, int) or isinstance(score, bool):
                raise ValidationError("score must be an integer in 1..5")
            if score < 1 or score > 5:
                raise ValidationError("score must be between 1 and 5")
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)

            rating = Rating(
                id=self._next_id(),
                rater_id=rater_id,
                ratee_id=ratee_id,
                score=score,
                feedback=feedback or "",
                task_id=task_id,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.performance._items[rating.id] = rating
            self.performance._by_ratee[ratee_id].append(rating)
            return deepcopy(rating)

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            ratings = self.performance._by_ratee.get(user_id, [])
            count = len(ratings)
            average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count if count else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "count": count,
                "average": average,
                "ratings": [deepcopy(r) for r in ratings],
            }

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._user_tasks(user_id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            in_progress = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
            )
            not_started = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
            )
            durations = self._completion_durations(tasks)
            average_time = (
                sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None
            )
            ratings = self.performance._by_ratee.get(user_id, [])
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                if rating_count
                else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "average_completion_time": average_time,
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
            }

    def _user_tasks(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        return [
            self.tasks._items[tid]
            for tid in self.tasks._by_assignee.get(user_id, set())
            if tid in self.tasks._items
        ]

    @staticmethod
    def _completion_durations(tasks: List[Task]) -> List[float]:
        durations = []
        for task in tasks:
            if (
                task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                and task.started_at is not None
                and task.completed_at is not None
            ):
                durations.append(
                    (task.completed_at - task.started_at).total_seconds()
                )
        return durations

    # ==================================================================
    # Reports
    # ==================================================================
    def project_progress_report(self, project_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            tasks = [
                self.tasks._items[tid]
                for tid in self.tasks._by_project.get(project_id, [])
                if tid in self.tasks._items
            ]
            total = len(tasks)
            status_counts = {status.value: 0 for status in TaskStatus}
            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1
            completed = status_counts[TaskStatus.COMPLETED.value]
            now = self._now()
            overdue = sum(
                1
                for task in tasks
                if task.deadline is not None
                and task.deadline < now
                and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            per_assignee: Dict[int, dict] = {}
            for task in tasks:
                if task.assignee_id is None:
                    continue
                bucket = per_assignee.setdefault(
                    task.assignee_id,
                    {"total_tasks": 0, "completed_tasks": 0},
                )
                bucket["total_tasks"] += 1
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    bucket["completed_tasks"] += 1
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee,
            }

    def team_performance_report(self, project_id: Optional[int] = None) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
                scope = [
                    self.tasks._items[tid]
                    for tid in self.tasks._by_project.get(project_id, [])
                    if tid in self.tasks._items
                ]
            else:
                scope = list(self.tasks._items.values())

            assignee_ids = sorted(
                {t.assignee_id for t in scope if t.assignee_id is not None}
            )
            rows = []
            for user_id in assignee_ids:
                user = self.users._items.get(user_id)
                user_tasks = [t for t in scope if t.assignee_id == user_id]
                rows.append(
                    self._performance_row(user_id, user, user_tasks)
                )
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "scope_project_id": project_id,
                "rows": rows,
            }

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = sorted(self._user_tasks(user_id), key=lambda t: t.id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            in_progress = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
            )
            not_started = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
            )
            durations = self._completion_durations(tasks)
            average_time = (
                sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None
            )
            user = self.users._items.get(user_id)
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "user_id": user_id,
                "username": user.username if user else None,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "average_completion_time": average_time,
                "tasks": [deepcopy(t) for t in tasks],
            }

    def _performance_row(
        self, user_id: int, user: Optional[User], tasks: List[Task]
    ) -> dict:
        total = len(tasks)
        completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
        durations = self._completion_durations(tasks)
        average_time = sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None
        ratings = self.performance._by_ratee.get(user_id, [])
        rating_count = len(ratings)
        rating_average = (
            sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
            if rating_count
            else 0.0
        )
        return {
            "user_id": user_id,
            "username": user.username if user else None,
            "total_tasks": total,
            "completed_tasks": completed,
            "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
            "average_completion_time": average_time,
            "rating_count": rating_count,
            "rating_average": rating_average,
        }

    # -- export ------------------------------------------------------------
    def export_report(self, report: dict, fmt: ReportFormat):
        """Export a report dict to CSV text or PDF bytes."""
        if not isinstance(report, dict):
            raise ValidationError("report must be a dict")
        if not isinstance(fmt, ReportFormat):
            raise ValidationError("fmt must be a ReportFormat")
        if fmt == ReportFormat.CSV:
            return self._report_to_csv(report)
        return self._report_to_pdf(report)

    def _report_to_csv(self, report: dict) -> str:
        out = io.StringIO()
        writer = csv.writer(out)
        rows = report.get("rows")
        if isinstance(rows, list) and rows and all(
            isinstance(row, dict) for row in rows
        ):
            header = list(rows[0].keys())
            writer.writerow(header)
            for row in rows:
                writer.writerow([self._cell(row.get(key)) for key in header])
        else:
            writer.writerow(["field", "value"])
            for key, value in report.items():
                writer.writerow([key, self._cell(value)])
        return out.getvalue()

    def _report_to_pdf(self, report: dict) -> bytes:
        lines = self._report_to_text(report)
        content_ops = ["BT", "/F1 12 Tf", "50 760 Td", "14 TL"]
        for index, line in enumerate(lines):
            text = self._pdf_escape(line)[:110]
            if index == 0:
                content_ops.append("({0}) Tj".format(text))
            else:
                content_ops.append("T* ({0}) Tj".format(text))
        content_ops.append("ET")
        content = "\n".join(content_ops).encode("latin-1", "replace")

        objects = [
            b"<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>",
            b"<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>",
            (
                b"<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
                b"/Resources << /Font << /F1 5 0 R >> >> /Contents 4 0 R >>"
            ),
            b"<< /Length "
            + str(len(content)).encode("ascii")
            + b" >>\nstream\n"
            + content
            + b"\nendstream",
            b"<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>",
        ]

        pdf = bytearray(b"%PDF-1.4\n")
        offsets = []
        for number, body in enumerate(objects, start=1):
            offsets.append(len(pdf))
            pdf += "{0} 0 obj\n".format(number).encode("ascii")
            pdf += body
            pdf += b"\nendobj\n"

        xref_pos = len(pdf)
        size = len(objects) + 1
        pdf += "xref\n0 {0}\n".format(size).encode("ascii")
        pdf += b"0000000000 65535 f \n"
        for offset in offsets:
            pdf += "{0:010d} 00000 n \n".format(offset).encode("ascii")
        pdf += b"trailer\n"
        pdf += "<< /Size {0} /Root 1 0 R >>\n".format(size).encode("ascii")
        pdf += b"startxref\n"
        pdf += "{0}\n".format(xref_pos).encode("ascii")
        pdf += b"%%EOF"
        return bytes(pdf)

    def _report_to_text(self, report: dict) -> List[str]:
        lines: List[str] = []
        title = str(report.get("report_type", "report"))
        lines.append("TeamSyncPro Report: {0}".format(title))
        rows = report.get("rows")
        for key, value in report.items():
            if key == "rows":
                continue
            lines.append("{0}: {1}".format(key, self._cell(value)))
        if isinstance(rows, list):
            lines.append("rows:")
            for row in rows:
                if isinstance(row, dict):
                    lines.append(
                        "  "
                        + ", ".join(
                            "{0}={1}".format(k, self._cell(v))
                            for k, v in row.items()
                        )
                    )
                else:
                    lines.append("  " + str(row))
        return lines

    @staticmethod
    def _cell(value):
        if value is None:
            return ""
        if isinstance(value, Enum):
            return value.value
        if isinstance(value, (dict, list, tuple, set)):
            return repr(value)
        return value

    @staticmethod
    def _pdf_escape(text: str) -> str:
        safe = "".join(ch if 32 <= ord(ch) < 127 else " " for ch in str(text))
        return safe.replace("\\", "\\\\").replace("(", "\\(").replace(")", "\\)")

    # ==================================================================
    # Validation helpers
    # ==================================================================
    @staticmethod
    def _has_text(value: Optional[str]) -> bool:
        return bool(value) and bool(str(value).strip())

    @classmethod
    def _clean_text(cls, value: str, label: str) -> str:
        if not cls._has_text(value):
            raise ValidationError("{0} must not be empty".format(label))
        return value.strip()

    @classmethod
    def _clean_username(cls, username: str) -> str:
        return cls._clean_text(username, "username")

    @classmethod
    def _clean_email(cls, email: str) -> str:
        if not cls._has_text(email):
            raise ValidationError("email must not be empty")
        cleaned = email.strip()
        if "@" not in cleaned or cleaned.startswith("@") or cleaned.endswith("@"):
            raise ValidationError("email must contain a local and domain part")
        return cleaned


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Optional non-interactive demonstration
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def _demo() -> None:  # pragma: no cover - illustrative only
    """Run a short end-to-end scenario and print a human-readable summary."""
    mgr = TeamCollaborationManager()
    alice = mgr.register_user("alice", "alice@example.com", UserRole.ADMIN)
    bob = mgr.register_user("bob", "bob@example.com")
    project = mgr.create_project(
        "Apollo",
        datetime(2024, 1, 1, 9, 0, 0),
        datetime(2024, 3, 1, 9, 0, 0),
        alice.id,
        "Launch the new collaboration suite.",
    )

    design = mgr.create_task(project.id, "Design schema", assignee_id=bob.id)
    mgr.update_status(design.id, TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
    mgr.update_status(design.id, TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
    mgr.create_task(project.id, "Build API", assignee_id=bob.id)

    mgr.post_message(alice.id, "Kickoff!", project_id=project.id)
    mgr.submit_rating(alice.id, bob.id, 5, "Great schema work.")

    report = mgr.project_progress_report(project.id)
    print("Project progress for '{0}':".format(project.name))
    print("  total tasks      :", report["total_tasks"])
    print("  status counts    :", report["status_counts"])
    print("  completion rate  : {0:.0%}".format(report["completion_rate"]))

    dash = mgr.get_user_dashboard(bob.id)
    print("Dashboard for {0}:".format(bob.username))
    print("  completed/total  : {0}/{1}".format(
        dash["completed_tasks"], dash["total_tasks"]
    ))
    print("  average rating   :", dash["rating_average"])
    print()
    print("CSV export (team performance):")
    print(mgr.export_report(mgr.team_performance_report(), ReportFormat.CSV))


if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover
    _demo()
